segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translation.text: 听录音，然后回答以下问题。

segments.text: What was the happy discovery?

translation.text: 那个令人高兴的发现是什么？

segments.text: Antique shops exert a peculiar fascination on a great many people.

translation.text: 古玩店对许多人有一种特殊的吸引力。

segments.text: The more expensive kind of antique shop, where rare objects are beautifully displayed in glass cases to keep them free from dust, is usually a forbidding place.

translation.text: 较为昂贵的古玩店把珍品精美地陈列在玻璃柜中以免落尘，通常令人望而却步。

segments.text: But no one has to muster up courage to enter a less pretentious antique shop.

translation.text: 但走进一家不那么气派的古玩店，谁也不必鼓足勇气。

segments.text: There is always hope that in its labyrinth of musty, dark, disordered rooms, a real rarity will be found amongst the piles of assorted junk that litter the floors.

translation.text: 在那迷宫般、霉味扑鼻、昏暗杂乱的房间里，人们总希望能从散落满地的各类破烂中发现真正的珍品。

segments.text: No one discovers a rarity by chance.

translation.text: 珍品不会偶然被发现。

segments.text: A truly dedicated bargain hunter must have patience, and above all, the ability to recognize the worth of something when he sees it.

translation.text: 真正执着的淘便宜货者必须有耐心，最重要的是一眼认出物品价值的能力。

segments.text: To do this, he must be at least as knowledgeable as the dealer.

translation.text: 为此，他至少得和古玩商一样博学。

segments.text: Like a scientist bent on making a discovery, he must cherish the hope that one day he will be amply rewarded.

translation.text: 他必须像一心想有所发现的科学家一样，始终抱着终有一天会得到丰厚回报的希望。

segments.text: My old friend, Halliday, is just such a person.

translation.text: 我的老朋友哈利迪正是这样的人。

segments.text: He has often described to me how he picked up a masterpiece for a mere fifty pounds.

translation.text: 他常向我讲述自己如何只花50英镑就买到了一幅杰作。

segments.text: One Saturday morning, Frank visited an antique shop in my neighborhood.

translation.text: 一个星期六的上午，弗兰克去了我家附近的一家古玩店。

segments.text: As he had never been there before, he found a great deal to interest him.

translation.text: 因为以前从未来过，他发现店里有许多东西令他感兴趣。

segments.text: The morning passed rapidly, and Frank was about to leave when he noticed a large packing case lying on the floor.

translation.text: 上午很快过去，弗兰克正准备离开时，注意到地板上放着一只大包装箱。

segments.text: The dealer told him that it had just come in, but that he could not be bothered to open it.

translation.text: 古玩商告诉他，箱子刚运来，但他懒得打开。

segments.text: Frank begged him to do so, and the dealer reluctantly prized it open.

translation.text: 弗兰克央求他打开，古玩商才不情愿地把箱子撬开。

segments.text: The contents were disappointing.

translation.text: 里面的东西令人失望。

segments.text: Apart from an interesting-looking carved dagger, the box was full of crockery, much of it broken.

translation.text: 除了一把看起来颇有意思的雕花匕首外，箱里全是陶器，而且大多已经破损。

segments.text: Frank gently lifted the crockery out of the box and suddenly noticed a miniature painting at the bottom of the packing case.

translation.text: 弗兰克轻轻地把陶器一件件拿出来，突然发现包装箱底部有一幅袖珍画。

segments.text: As its composition and line reminded him of an Italian painting he knew well, he decided to buy it.

translation.text: 画的构图和线条使他想起一幅很熟悉的意大利画作，于是他决定买下它。

segments.text: Glancing at it briefly, the dealer told him that it was worth fifty pounds.

translation.text: 古玩商匆匆看了一眼，说它值50英镑。

segments.text: Frank could hardly conceal his excitement, for he knew that he had made a real discovery.

translation.text: 弗兰克几乎掩饰不住自己的兴奋，因为他知道自己确实有了重大发现。

segments.text: The tiny painting proved to be an unknown masterpiece by Correggio, and was worth hundreds of thousands of pounds.

translation.text: 这幅小画后来被证实是柯勒乔的一幅不为人知的杰作，价值数十万英镑。
